challenges.title: String Methods
challenges.description: Given text = '  Hello, World!  ', create four variables: 'stripped' (whitespace removed from both ends), 'upper' (stripped text converted to uppercase), 'replaced' (in stripped text, replace 'Hello' with 'Python'), and 'words' (split the stripped text by spaces).
challenges.starter_code:
text = '  Hello, World!  '

# Remove leading/trailing whitespace
stripped = # your code here

# Convert stripped to uppercase
upper = # your code here

# Replace 'Hello' with 'Python' in stripped
replaced = # your code here

# Split stripped into a list of words
words = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert stripped == 'Hello, World!', f"stripped should be 'Hello, World!', got '{stripped}'"
assert upper == 'HELLO, WORLD!', f"upper should be 'HELLO, WORLD!', got '{upper}'"
assert replaced == 'Python, World!', f"replaced should be 'Python, World!', got '{replaced}'"
assert words == ['Hello,', 'World!'], f"words should be ['Hello,', 'World!'], got {words}"
hints: Use .strip() to remove whitespace from both ends
Use .split() on the stripped result to split by whitespace

challenges.title: F-String Formatter
challenges.description: Given name = 'Alice', age = 30, and score = 95.5, create a formatted string called 'report' that reads exactly: 'Name: Alice | Age: 30 | Score: 95.50' (note: score must show exactly 2 decimal places).
challenges.starter_code:
name = 'Alice'
age = 30
score = 95.5

# Create the formatted report string
report = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert report == 'Name: Alice | Age: 30 | Score: 95.50', f"Expected 'Name: Alice | Age: 30 | Score: 95.50', got '{report}'"
assert isinstance(report, str), 'report should be a string'
hints: Use an f-string: f"Name: {name} | Age: {age} | Score: {score}"
To format score with 2 decimal places, use {score:.2f}
The :.2f format specifier means 'float with 2 decimal places'

challenges.title: String Slicer
challenges.description: Given s = 'abcdefghij', create three variables using slicing: 'reversed_s' (the entire string reversed), 'every_other' (every other character starting from index 0), and 'middle' (characters at index 3 through 6 inclusive).
challenges.starter_code:
s = 'abcdefghij'

# Reverse the entire string
reversed_s = # your code here

# Get every other character (index 0, 2, 4, ...)
every_other = # your code here

# Get characters at index 3 through 6 inclusive
middle = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert reversed_s == 'jihgfedcba', f"reversed_s should be 'jihgfedcba', got '{reversed_s}'"
assert every_other == 'acegi', f"every_other should be 'acegi', got '{every_other}'"
assert middle == 'defg', f"middle should be 'defg', got '{middle}'"
hints: Every other character uses a step of 2: s[::2]
Remember s[3:7] gets indices 3, 4, 5, 6 (end index is exclusive)
